code_scope.line_range: N/A - Target codebase is Nimbus (CL), property targets EL
code_scope.notes: This property pertains to Execution Layer blob transaction validation in the mempool. Nimbus is a Consensus Layer client. The validation of equal counts of versioned_hashes, blobs, and commitments occurs in EL clients (Geth, Reth, Nethermind, etc.) when receiving blob transactions via P2P or RPC. CL clients like Nimbus validate blob sidecars for blocks but do not validate individual transactions in the EL mempool.

summary: Property targets EL blob transaction mempool validation; Nimbus is a CL client without EL transaction pool validation logic.

phase1_abstract_interpretation.summary: Scope analysis: Property PROP-W5-EIP7594-BLOBTX-PRECOND-002 requires validating equality of versioned_hashes.length == blobs.length == commitments.length for blob transactions. This validation occurs at the Execution Layer (EL) transaction pool during transaction ingestion. Nimbus is a Consensus Layer (CL) client that validates blob sidecars attached to beacon blocks, not raw transactions in the EL mempool.

phase2_symbolic_execution.summary: Cannot perform symbolic execution on target codebase (Nimbus CL) as the required validation logic resides in Execution Layer clients (Geth, Reth, Nethermind, Besu). The ACTION-VALIDATE-QUANTITY-MATCH from SUBGRAPH-EIP7594-BLOB-TRANSACTION-VALIDATION is an EL-side validation that is not present in CL clients.

phase2_5_reachability_analysis.summary: N/A - Cannot perform reachability analysis on EL validation logic within a CL client codebase.
phase2_5_reachability_analysis.notes: The validation logic for blob transaction quantity matching is not present in Nimbus (CL). EL clients perform this validation when receiving blob transactions via P2P (eth/69 or later) or JSON-RPC (eth_sendRawTransaction). Nimbus only validates blob sidecars for beacon blocks, which have already passed EL validation.

phase3_invariant_proving.summary: Cannot prove or disprove the invariant within Nimbus codebase as the relevant validation logic is not present.

phase3_5_scope_filtering.recommendation: This property should be audited against Execution Layer client implementations (e.g., go-ethereum/core/txpool, reth transaction validation, nethermind transaction pool). The Ethereum Bug Bounty program covers both EL and CL clients, but this check must be performed on the appropriate client type.
phase3_5_scope_filtering.notes: To audit this property correctly: (1) Identify the EL client target (Geth, Reth, Nethermind, Besu); (2) Locate blob transaction validation code in the transaction pool; (3) Verify that len(tx.BlobVersionedHashes) == len(tx.BlobKZGCommitments) == len(tx.Blobs) is enforced before accepting transactions into the mempool. Example location in Geth: core/txpool/validation.go or similar blob transaction validation logic.

scope_mismatch.recommendation: Re-assign this property to an EL client audit queue (Geth, Reth, Nethermind, Besu)
